HGSS.267.0000:
Welcome!\f
From here, you can select a Pokémon
from your PC Box and send it to the\r
Pokéwalker to take along on a Stroll.\f
HGSS.267.0001:
Please select the Pokémon
you’d like to take along.
HGSS.267.0003:
Caution!\f
All data saved on the Pokéwalker will
be erased.\f
Before erasing it, please be sure to
confirm the information.\f
The Pokémon on your Pokéwalker will
return, but all other data on the\r
Pokéwalker will be erased.\f
Erase the data on the Pokéwalker?
HGSS.267.0004:
All records on the
Pokéwalker will be erased.
HGSS.267.0005:
Point at the DS Card
and select “CONNECT.”
HGSS.267.0007:
In a place where it’s OK for
the Pokéwalker to emit\r
noise, point it at the\r
DS Card, press the button,\r
and wait five seconds.
HGSS.267.0008:
Your Pokémon can now take
a Stroll in the Pokéwalker.
HGSS.267.0009: Carry over the Pokéwalker Step Count?
HGSS.267.0011:
Please select a Pokémon from your
PC Box.\f
HGSS.267.0012:
Select the Route you would
like to take a Stroll on.\f
HGSS.267.0013:
Saving...
Do not turn off the power.
HGSS.267.0015:
To use the Pokéwalker, you must have
at least five open spaces in a PC Box.\f
Please come back once you have opened
at least five spaces.\f
HGSS.267.0016:
The Nintendo DS will connect to
the Pokéwalker.[0201,005A]
HGSS.267.0018:
Caution!\f
You can send the Pokémon you are out
on a Stroll with back, but will lose the\r
ability to transfer any other Pokémon\r
on the Pokéwalker.\f
Send the Pokémon back anyway?
HGSS.267.0019:
The Pokémon out on a Stroll
will be sent back.
HGSS.267.0020:
Any received Pokémon were released
back into the wild.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0021:
Take a Stroll on the
[3413,0000,0000] Route?
HGSS.267.0022:
Welcome!\f
From here, you can select a Pokémon
from your PC Box and send it to the\r
Pokéwalker to take along on a Stroll.\f
Please come back once you have placed
at least one Pokémon in a PC Box.\f
HGSS.267.0025:
The Pokéwalker has been registered to
this DS Card. When sending back your\r
Pokémon, please return it to\r
this DS Card.\f
Please enjoy your Stroll with your
Pokémon!\f
HGSS.267.0033:
Canceled the connection to
the Pokéwalker.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0034:
This Pokéwalker has already
been registered.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0036:
This Pokéwalker has not yet
been registered.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0037:
There is already a Pokémon on
this Pokéwalker.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0038:
This Pokéwalker is not the
registered Pokéwalker.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0039:
This Pokéwalker is not the
registered Pokéwalker.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0041:
Connection to the Pokéwalker has
been suspended.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0042:
Connection was disrupted. Please try
your connection again.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0043:
There are not any Pokémon on
this Pokéwalker.[0201,0078]
HGSS.267.0046: Head out for another Stroll sometime!\f
HGSS.267.0047:
Stroll for a bit with the Pokémon you’ve
sent to the Pokéwalker!\f
HGSS.267.0048:
...
What?\f
The collected Watts have unlocked
something on the Pokéwalker!\f
A new Route has been found on the
Pokéwalker!\f
HGSS.267.0049:
All Pokémon you caught on the Stroll
have been sent to the PC Box!\f
HGSS.267.0050:
Any items found on the Stroll have been
placed in your Bag!\f
HGSS.267.0052:
[0101,0001,0000]’s level went up one from
the Stroll!\f
HGSS.267.0053:
Congratulations!\f
The Pokéwalker has reached
9,999,999 steps!\f
HGSS.267.0054:
Received a Starf Berry as a reward!\f
Placed the reward in the Bag!\f
HGSS.267.0077: Save, then go for a Stroll.
HGSS.267.0086:
Watts required to find the next
Route: [0138,000C,0000] W
HGSS.267.0087:
It seems that another Route will
appear soon.
HGSS.267.0088:
You can select where you’d like to take a
Stroll from the many Routes.
HGSS.267.0119:
A wonderfully refreshing field
for a Stroll. You can meet many
kinds of Pokémon here.
HGSS.267.0120:
A large natural forest that has
been allowed to grow. Many
Pokémon live deep in the forest.
HGSS.267.0121:
A hilly, rugged mountain road.
The view from atop the mountain
is very beautiful.
HGSS.267.0122:
This beautiful sea is a popular
place for a Stroll. You can meet
many Water-type Pokémon.
HGSS.267.0123:
A residential area somewhere.
Even when you take a Stroll
here, you can meet Pokémon.
HGSS.267.0124:
A dark cave sometimes used as a
test of courage. Look closely,
and you’ll find many Pokémon.
HGSS.267.0125:
A deep, blue lake. It’s said that
some rare Pokémon live at the
bottom of this lake.
HGSS.267.0126:
The outskirts of a town. Where
people have moved away,
Pokémon have moved in.
HGSS.267.0127:
A Route that goes deep into the
Hoenn region. You can meet many
Pokémon not in Johto or Kanto.
HGSS.267.0128:
A very warm beach in the Hoenn
region. You can meet many
Pokémon not in Johto or Kanto.
HGSS.267.0129:
A path through a currently
active volcano. Very fiery and
rare Pokémon live here.
HGSS.267.0130:
A path that works with the
trees of the forest. Pokémon
here are always changing.
HGSS.267.0131:
A pitch-black cave that seems
haunted. It’s said that many
Pokémon live deep inside.
HGSS.267.0132:
A Route through the Sinnoh
region, far from Johto and
Kanto. Rare Pokémon live here.
HGSS.267.0133:
A snow-covered road where a
misstep could result in a fall--
it tests your trust in Pokémon.
HGSS.267.0134:
A forest surrounded by many old
trees since the olden days.
Many Pokémon live here.
HGSS.267.0135:
A lake concealed in white mist.
It lends a very mysterious feel
to the area.
HGSS.267.0136:
Strong waves wash onto this
beach. Because people don’t
visit, rare Pokémon live here.
HGSS.267.0137:
A high-class resort everyone
wants to visit, famous for the
cute Pokémon living here.
HGSS.267.0138:
A quiet, deep, and empty cave.
If you look carefully, you’ll find
some rare Pokémon.
HGSS.267.0139:
A small island far away on the
horizon. If you look to the sea,
you can see Pokémon swimming.
HGSS.267.0140:
A dark mountain path that gives
you the feeling you’ve traveled
through time back to the past.
HGSS.267.0141:
Many PIKACHU live in harmony in
this forest. Walk through here
frequently, and try to find one!
HGSS.267.0142:
A fun Route to play with your
Pokéwalker. Take a Stroll and
visit the many places!
HGSS.267.0143:
You can find many Pokémon on
this Route. Take a long walk and
see if you can find them all!
HGSS.267.0144:
If you’re aiming to be Champion,
you’ll want to walk this Route!
You’ll meet strong Pokémon!
HGSS.267.0145:
You can meet cute Pokémon that
evolve through friendship and
find items to evolve Pokémon!
